reference_check.content:
Ok — compare your blaster with this reference.

Check:
• mould shape
• rear bump length
• plastic colour
• detail sharpness

Can you identify it against the reference image?

Reply with:

1 Yes
2 No
3 Not sure

images.title: Jawa Blaster Reference
images.caption: Compare mould shape, rear bump length, plastic colour and detail sharpness.

float_test.content:
Next check: float test.

• If it sinks, treat it as a reproduction
• If it floats, it may be original

Important:
Modern reproductions can float, so this is not proof.

Does yours sink or float?

Reply with:

1 Float
2 Sink

sink_result.content:
If it sinks, treat it as a reproduction.

Original vintage Jawa blasters should float, although floating alone does not prove originality.

colour_check.content:
Good. Since it floats, next check the colour.

Most original Jawa blasters are dark blue or black-blue tones.

What colour does yours look like?

Reply with:

1 Dark blue / black-blue
2 Black
3 Grey
4 Silver
5 Not sure

dark_blue_result.content:
That is the safest colour range for most original Jawa blasters.

Known original colours include dark-blue and black-blue tones, with some semi-translucent blueish, greenish or greyish variation.

Still compare the exact mould shape, rear bump and detail sharpness against Variant Villain:
https://www.variantvillain.com/accessory-guide/jawa-blaster/

If you want to narrow the mould next, tell me whether the rear bump is long, short or missing.

black_light_check.content:
Black needs caution.

It could be:
• a reproduction
• a modern accessory
• a very dark blue original being mistaken for black
• or, less commonly, a Brazilian Glasslite black blaster

The legitimate black Glasslite version uses the M2 mould family, so compare it especially against the M2 version:
https://www.variantvillain.com/accessory-guide/jawa-blaster/#m2

Next check: place the blaster against a strong light.

Do the edges look blue when backlit?

Reply with:

1 Yes, the edges look blue
2 No, it stays black
3 Not sure

black_edges_blue_result.content:
That suggests it may actually be a very dark blue or black-blue blaster rather than a true black one.

That is more in line with common original Jawa blaster colours, but you still need to confirm the mould.

Next compare the rear bump:
• long bump = M1
• short bump = M2
• no bump = M3

Use the full guide here:
https://www.variantvillain.com/accessory-guide/jawa-blaster/

If you want, tell me whether the rear bump is long, short or missing and I’ll help narrow it down.

black_edges_not_blue_result.content:
If it stays black under strong light, be cautious.

A legitimate black Jawa blaster is most likely the Brazilian Glasslite version, which belongs to the M2 mould family.

So the key question is whether it matches M2 traits:
• short rear bump
• clean, sharper mould detail
• scar to the top right of the bump

If it does not match M2, treat it as suspicious rather than original.

Compare carefully here:
https://www.variantvillain.com/accessory-guide/jawa-blaster/#m2

black_edges_unsure_result.content:
No problem. Black and very dark blue can be hard to judge.

Best next step:
• check it in daylight
• place it against a strong light
• look for blue on the edges
• then compare the mould against M2 if it still appears black

M2 reference:
https://www.variantvillain.com/accessory-guide/jawa-blaster/#m2

If you want, tell me the rear bump shape: long, short or none.

grey_result.content:
Grey pieces need extra caution.

Grey is a very common reproduction colour for Jawa blasters.

There are rare silver Glasslite versions, but they are extremely uncommon and should not be assumed.

Best next step: compare it closely with the full Variant Villain guide:
https://www.variantvillain.com/accessory-guide/jawa-blaster/

silver_result.content:
Silver is unusual.

There is a rare silver Jawa blaster associated with Brazilian Glasslite production, but it is extremely uncommon.

Do not assume it is Glasslite without matching the mould and provenance carefully.

Use the full reference here:
https://www.variantvillain.com/accessory-guide/jawa-blaster/

not_sure_colour_result.content:
If you are not sure on colour, compare it in daylight and against a strong light.

Most originals fall into dark blue / black-blue tones, plus rare Glasslite black and silver versions.

Full reference:
https://www.variantvillain.com/accessory-guide/jawa-blaster/
